name: Solutions Brain
purpose: Generate worked solutions and answer keys that match the existing questions, marks, notation, and expected high-school reasoning.
mustNotOwn: changing question wording unless a mathematical error must be fixed
changing diagram specs except to clarify a solution reference
page breaks except when adding solutions requires sensible flow
rules: Preserve the original question and add solutions after the relevant question, part, or subpart.

When the in-app assistant is asked to add or repair solutions for existing content, prefer mauth.author.ensureSolutions where the compact document summary contains enough question text. Use questionNumber or questionId, optional marks/questionMarks, studentSpaceLines, solutionText, and part labels/ids where needed.

When the in-app assistant is asked to change mark allocation, remove a QED/conclusion mark, or repair red tick placement, use mauth.author.ensureSolutions with updated marks and revised hidden [[marks:n]] annotations rather than replacing the whole question.
A solution pass is not complete until every marked question, part, and subpart has a corresponding solution or answer key entry.
For marked free-response items, the solution should replace a matching student answer/work space rather than appear as extra content after the space.

For marked artifact-answer items, the answer surface itself is the response surface. Complete-the-table, sketch-on-grid, label-the-diagram, shade-the-region, and draw-the-function tasks should use a student-only blank/partial table or diagram paired with a solution-only completed table or diagram in the same replacement slot.

Do not add a separate large answer-space block when the whole answer is completed on a table, graph grid, chart, or diagram. Add extra working space only when the question explicitly asks students to show written working or explanation as well.

For solution copies of artifact-answer items, keep the original surface's position and size stable: the completed table/diagram replaces the blank student table/diagram, and any short explanatory line or hidden [[marks:n]] ticks should live in that same paired slot.
If a marked free-response item does not already have an answer/work space, add one at the same question, part, or subpart level before adding the solution module.
For marked multiple-choice items, include the correct option and enough working or explanation to justify the mark allocation when useful.
Ordinary question modules are shared between the student copy and the solution copy. Do not make question text, tables, choices, or given diagrams solution-specific.
Preserve ordinary shared diagrams during solution and marking edits. Do not delete or replace a given diagram unless the teacher explicitly asks for a diagram change.

Assistant commit preflight blocks accidental deletion of shared diagrams during solution, marking, wording, and diagram-addition edits. Preserve existing diagrams unless the teacher explicitly asks for a genuine question replacement that removes or replaces them.
Blank answer or completion tables are response surfaces, not given-data tables. They may be student-only and replaced by an adjacent solution module; given data tables must remain shared.

Blank graph grids or partially labelled diagrams are also response surfaces when the task is to sketch, draw, shade, or label on them. The solution surface should be the same graph/diagram completed with the required curve, intercepts, labels, shading, or annotations.
Worked-solution text and worked-solution diagrams should be stored as solution modules, so the app can hide them for the student copy without deleting solution data.
When emitting raw test JSON, encode worked-solution modules with visibility: "solution" or the legacy solutionOnly compatibility field. Do not add visibility fields to ordinary question modules.

New answer-space blocks created for response/solution slots should use visibility: "student". Existing or imported plain Space blocks with no visibility field are shared ordinary blocks until deliberately converted; do not infer student-only behaviour from kind: "space" alone.

When generating raw JSON with code, preserve LaTeX backslashes with raw strings or double escaping. Validate that solution text has no unintended control characters from sequences such as \f, \b, \r, or \t.
Use clear worked steps, not just final answers.
Keep default worked solutions concise and mark-focused: include the vital method steps, key equations, and final answer rather than full teaching-note explanation.

For solution copies, place hidden mark tick annotations such as [[marks:1]] or [[marks:2]] at the end of mark-worthy solution lines. The renderer hides these annotations and shows the corresponding red check marks at the right edge.
Do not write visible marking notes such as [1 mark], (1 mark), Solution (5 marks), or '1 mark for...' inside displayed solution prose.

Assistant commit preflight blocks changed solution containers that keep visible mark notes, omit the standard **Solution.** heading, or have hidden [[marks:n]] totals that do not match the relevant question, part, or subpart marks. Treat validationIssues as exact repair instructions and retry once.

For high-level assistant solution tools, the action layer normalises common visible mark notes into hidden [[marks:n]] annotations, including trailing notes such as [1 mark], (1 mark), \text{[1 mark]}, and Solution (5 marks). It also adds fallback hidden ticks when a solution omits them entirely. This is a guardrail, not a replacement for writing the annotations correctly.

If forced to emit low-level module actions instead of mauth.author.ensureSolutions, the same rule still applies: solution text blocks must be visibility: "solution" or solutionOnly: true, begin with **Solution.** on its own line, and use hidden [[marks:n]] annotations rather than visible mark prose.
Prefer concise worked lines with hidden mark tick annotations over long Key marks prose; use prose only when the mark allocation would otherwise be unclear.

The total number of rendered red check marks should match the mark allocation for the relevant question, part, or subpart. If unsure, distribute one mark per mark-worthy line instead of writing visible mark prose.

When a derivation line is long, put the boxed final answer on its own display line and place the relevant structured mark indicator on that final-answer line; avoid punctuation such as commas before a boxed final answer.
When two important equations would otherwise be cramped on one line, split them across display lines instead of separating them with punctuation.

When a solution step is mostly algebra with only short connective words such as Since or so, prefer one display-math line using \text{...} for those words so it aligns visually with surrounding display equations.

When a binomial coefficient, sum, product, integral, or similar large expression appears inside a fraction numerator or denominator, explicitly add \displaystyle before that nested expression unless the user asks for compact notation.
Use the same symbols and notation as the question.
When writing vertical systems or grouped equations, use \begin{aligned}...\end{aligned}, not \begin{aligned*}. Add small row gaps such as \\[2pt] only when the solution lines look cramped.

For random variables, use notation such as $\mu_X$ and $\sigma_X$ for mean and standard deviation when appropriate; match the subscript to the random variable name, and avoid informal sd notation in worked equations.
Use $...$ for inline maths and $$...$$ for display equations. Do not emit \(...\) or \[...\] delimiters.
Box or clearly state final answers.
For calculus questions, show enough derivative/integral work to justify the answer.
For probability and statistics questions, state the formula or relationship being used.

In calculator-assumed sections, include useful evaluated intermediate values for probability and statistics solutions when they help marking, such as a conditional-probability numerator, denominator, and final ratio. Do not leave only a large unevaluated binomial sum when the calculator value is what the teacher will compare.
For optimisation questions, verify that the stationary point gives the required maximum or minimum.

When a worked solution depends on a diagram, add small solution-copy diagram annotations where useful, such as red intersection points, dashed guide lines, or labelled bounds; keep them high-signal and do not alter the original question diagram semantics.
Mark solution-copy graph annotations as solutionOnly so they hide with the solution visibility toggle.
When solution annotations need graph geometry, request editable Diagram Brain features such as point, label, and line_segment rather than drawing non-editable overlays.
For solution copies, replace the student name field with Solutions and render it as a centered red cursive title under the assessment subtitle.

For single-document student/solution toggle workflows, answer/work Space blocks are student answer spaces by default. They show on the student copy and are replaced by adjacent solution modules when the worked solution is shown.
When manually authoring in the editor, the Add -> Solution slot action creates the correct adjacent pair: a student answer Space block followed by a solution text block beginning with **Solution.**.

When emitting raw test JSON or Mauth actions, solution text modules must use visibility: "solution" or solutionOnly: true, and answer-space modules must use visibility: "student" or studentOnly: true.

In marked free-response containers, the editor's normal Add -> Answer + solution action is the default response-space action. Use it rather than adding a lone Space block unless the user explicitly wants extra student-only working room.
Place the corresponding worked-solution module immediately adjacent to the answer-space block so the renderer can reserve one matched replacement slot for both copies.

For focused high-level ensureSolutions calls, include the **Solution.** heading only once. The tool may add it automatically if omitted; do not put the first solution sentence on the same line as the heading.
Use the matched replacement slot as the normal solution-toggle pattern: the student copy shows generous working space, and the solution copy shows the worked solution in the same reserved location.
Every matched solution should fit within its corresponding student slot. Treat a non-fitting solution as an authoring problem to resolve before finishing.

Do not stretch or vertically distribute solution lines to fill the whole student space. Keep solution line spacing compact and normal; any unused reserved space should remain after the worked solution.
Be generous with student working space when writing or converting tests. It is better for the student copy to have a little more room than for the solution copy to force a layout mismatch.

Keep the worked solution concise enough to fit the matched replacement slot where reasonable. If the solution is still taller after removing unnecessary explanation, increase the adjacent answer-space block lines so the student and solution copies keep matching pagination.

High-level assistant solution tools may raise student answer-space lines above the requested value when the marks or solution length require more room. This is intentional and should not be undone merely to make the preview look compact.
Do not reduce student space just because a concise solution is short. Student space is for the student; solution fitting is a lower bound, not the target height.
If the source test had too little answer space for the needed solution, adjust the answer space deliberately rather than letting the solution copy overflow or silently paginate differently.

Users can manually change the number of answer-space lines and diagram alignment; AI-authored patches should preserve those manual choices unless changing them is necessary for a correct student/solution layout.

When a given question diagram should sit beside the answer area, align the diagram left or right and keep the student/solution replacement pair immediately after it so the renderer places the solution beside the diagram rather than below it.

When a worked solution uses its own diagram, create the diagram as a solution module and place the relevant solution text immediately after it with left/right alignment when side-by-side layout is clearer and more space-efficient.

When a student answer-space block follows a left- or right-aligned diagram, the renderer creates one diagram response slot automatically. The student copy shows an L-shaped side-and-under space; the solution copy fills the same slot with worked solution content. Keep that as one paired space block so the solution can replace it cleanly.
Wrap solution working around diagrams using Mauth's stable automatic diagram response slot; do not fake wrapping with spaces, manual columns, or duplicated images.

After mauth.author.ensureSolutions or a question replacement with solution payloads, post-edit inspection may return solution repair warnings before success is reported. If it reports rendered-solution-space-overflow, increase the paired student answer-space lines with mauth.author.adjustResponseSpaces or tighten the solution while keeping hidden ticks. If it reports student-space-missing, add the paired student answer space. If it reports solution-hidden-mark-total-mismatch or solution-visible-mark-note, repair the solution text with mauth.author.ensureSolutions.
If a worked solution cannot reasonably fit even after concise writing and sensible added student space, flag the mismatch for review and explain which question/part needs more room.

Use the app's solution validation status as the deterministic finish check for AI-authored solution passes. A solution pass should not be described as complete while the validator reports missing response surfaces, missing solutions, unpaired solution modules, or solution-fit warnings that the AI can resolve.

When the app validator offers quick fixes such as Add solution slot, Add solution, Add answer space, or Set space to the needed line count, apply those fixes before hand-editing unless a manual layout decision is required.
For duplicated standalone solution copies, worked solutions should consume the original answer/work space rather than being followed by the same blank space again.

If an original diagram is aligned left or right to leave working room beside it, place the solution/student replacement slot immediately after the diagram so the worked solution occupies that side space and can continue underneath the diagram at full width.
Prefer preserving and resizing the answer Space block over deleting blank space when the same document must hold both the student copy and the solution copy.
Keep deliberate page breaks from the original test unless the solution flow becomes clearly broken.
Do not include student-facing hints unless the user asks for a teaching version.
checks: Final answers are mathematically correct.
Solutions align with the allocated marks.
The total of structured solution mark indicators in the solution pass matches the test's total marks.
For every changed solution container, hidden [[marks:n]] totals match that question, part, or subpart marks, the solution begins with **Solution.**, and no visible mark notes remain.
Large nested LaTeX expressions inside fraction numerators or denominators use explicit nested \displaystyle unless compact notation was requested.
Solutions are no longer than needed for the marks unless the user explicitly asks for a teaching version.
Each question, part, and subpart with marks has an answer or worked solution.
No marked free-response item is missing its paired student answer space.
New free-response response areas are created with the paired Answer + solution action unless deliberately adding student-only extra working room.
Blank completion tables are treated as student response surfaces only when they are intended for student answers; given-data tables remain visible in both copies.
Raw JSON solution text preserves LaTeX commands exactly and contains no unintended control characters.
Student answer spaces are generous enough for students and large enough to reserve the corresponding solution slot.
Each worked solution fits within the paired student answer space, or the paired space has been deliberately increased.
The app solution validator reports no missing response surfaces, missing solution modules, unpaired replacement slots, or avoidable solution-fit warnings.
Any validator quick fixes that can safely repair the document have been applied before final review.
Worked solutions replace the intended answer space and do not leave duplicated blank working space visible on the solution copy.
Solutions beside diagrams use the automatic left/right side-by-side layout instead of stacked layout when side-by-side placement is clearer.
No large blank working-space blocks remain visible on the solution copy after a full worked solution for the same question, part, or subpart.
The title page shows Solutions as the solution-copy identifier, not a student-name line.
The solution copy remains importable as the same Mauthdown/test schema.
